question: Does the compressed-only union dKV split materialize when measured against the actual full shared-KV attention contract with local-window plus compressed indices?
read: The compressed-only split can beat the compressed-only retained gate, but the actual full shared-KV contract includes a 128-token local window plus 512 compressed LI groups.
Once dQ is computed over all 640 selected keys and local dKV is added, the split boundary is 13.32 ms, slower than the retained 11.13 ms sparse-attention backward gate.
This closes the immediate materialization branch: launching separate dQ/local-dKV/compressed-union-dKV kernels is not a valid TorchTitan/e2e promotion path.

The next viable sparse-attention kernel has to share the QK/P/scaled-dP producer across full dQ, local dKV, and compressed group-aware dKV, ideally in a HIP/CK/rocWMMA cooperative owner rather than a multi-launch Triton split.
next: Do not wire this full split into TorchTitan as the promoted backend; it would likely reduce e2e TFLOPs.

Use the full-contract numbers to size the cooperative kernel: recover about 2.2-2.9 ms by eliminating repeated producer work and the local+compressed dKV add before this branch beats the retained gate materially.
Continue the low-level cooperative owner path where the score tile is consumed before HBM and dKV epilogue is group-aware within the same producer.
